focus: Lineage write boundary enforced by prompt text only

findingDetails.title: Lineage write boundary is enforced by prompt text, not a path-scoped sandbox
findingDetails.claim: fanout-run.cjs:1287-1293 documents that the lineageDir-only write boundary is enforced by the prompt instruction ("Do not touch any path outside lineageDir") rather than by a narrower sandbox; the sandbox defaults to write-capable so the review subprocess can write its own artifacts. A malformed or non-compliant executor can therefore write outside lineageDir with no OS-level barrier. This is a defense-in-depth gap, not an active exploit.
findingDetails.recommendation: When the CLIs expose a path-scoped workspace-write mode, switch the default for review/research lineages to it; until then document the prompt-only boundary as a known limitation in the fanout security note.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Finding is within the declared spec-folder / deep-loop orchestration review scope.
